lines.text: New face, huh? Come on in. Sit down, relax. No need to look so nervous. I'm the... acting manager of this Interstellar Dispatch Office for the time being.
You ever heard of the Galactic HR Department?
Great. Then I'll keep it short. We've had a flood of alien job seekers lately. Whole crowds of them.
They're sick of their home planets, bored out of their skulls, and looking for somewhere new to work.
No worries. Honestly, they mostly just send emails.
Long story short, alien job seekers have been pouring in lately.
They're tired of life back home and want a fresh start. New planet, new job, same old trouble.
The problem is, some of them have tempers hot enough to melt steel.
Some are dumb as asteroids.
And some of them... well.
Let's just say they've eaten coworkers before.
Our job is to screen them, sort them, and send them to planets where they're least likely to cause disasters.
Do it well, and headquarters might even throw a bonus your way.
Next up, your senior coworker Woofie will teach you the ropes.
That's the rundown. Good luck, kiddo.
Try not to get eaten.Ciao~
Hey there, rookie! I'm Woofie, your senior guide around here. Woof!
This right here is your workstation.
Any creatures needing placement will show up at the window. Woof!
You can check their [PROFILE] on the tablet below.
Use the tabs to switch screens and inspect whatever they're carrying in their [PACKAGE].
These are the scanning buttons. Woof!
Tap [SCAN] to check their external features, or [X-RAY] to get a look at their internal skeleton.
Now then, a proper dispatcher also needs to ask a few simple questions.
That's what the [ASK] button is for.
After that, you can review their answers in the [PROFILE] tab.
Oh, and one important thing—if you haven't examined them properly first, the [ASK] button won't work. Woof!
Once you think you've got them figured out, hit [DISPATCH] and choose the planet that suits them best.
I believe in you, rookie! Woof!
Oh! Looks like you survived your first day on the job.
Let me see that performance report...
[n] satisfied, [n] average, [n] complaints.
Well, well. You're sharper than you look.
Hm. You're taking this seriously. I like that.
Looks like you've still got a lot to learn.
Good grief. Were you making decisions with your elbows?
I knew I wasn't wrong about you.
Talented inspectors are getting harder to find these days.
Keep this up, and you might even earn yourself a promotion to headquarters before long.
Confidence is good. Keep that fire in you.
Just don't slack off on your training.
And ask your senior, Woofie, plenty of questions while you can.
Hah. Got jokes, huh?
Still, for a rookie to make it this far already... not bad at all.
Oh please, don't try that cute routine on me.
I've seen every trick in the book.
Hit the books tonight, or next time I'm shipping you off to Planet Dummy.
Alright, that's enough for today.
No matter what kind of work you do, remember to eat well, stay hydrated, and leave work on time.
See you tomorrow！ Ciao~

1.name: Budya
1.description: An extraterrestrial paradise born from pure, innocent hearts. A world composed of harmless flora and fauna.
1.planetneed: Everyone is welcome to join OVO!

2.name: Tonis
2.description: Highly commercialized. The designated supplier for the Interstellar Alliance.
2.planetneed: Hiring hair stylists. Speak with your hands, come quickly!

3.name: Shawshank
3.description: Over 80% of the planet is ocean. The only landmass hosts the galaxy's most heavily guarded maximum-security prison.
3.planetneed:
Urgent need for 10 maintenance workers.
“The highest bounty for an escaped prisoner is 503,333…”

4.name: Screw
4.description: A collective of intelligent mechanical spirals. The entire planet has been transformed into a massive starship.
4.planetneed:
Hiring crew members.
Old exploration-type robots welcome. Only the core needs to be intact.
The ship has ample space and no wear-and-tear issues.

5.name: Arrakis
5.description: Once rich with immense energy sources, now a lawless paradise for outlaws after drying up.
5.planetneed: It's a Sicilian message. It means Luca Brasi sleeps withthe fishes.Send it and you take the money.

6.name: Lost Eden
6.description: A garden of Eden-like bliss. If you can imagine it, you can have endless joy here.
6.planetneed: Work? No, come play. Your only responsibility is to make yourself happy — and feel free to tell others how to be even happier.

7.name: Jelly
7.description: The planet's surface is covered in gelatinous material. The favorite amusement park destination for interstellar families with children.
7.planetneed: Hiring a Jelly Pit Rescue Assistant. Rescue stuck kids, keep them smiling, stay cheerful. Free park access included.

8.name: Cinnabar Eye
8.description: The atmosphere consists of hydrogen and helium. The largest gas giant in the Kartos Star System.
8.planetneed:
Position: Lead Scout
Duties: Explore uncharted areas and collect resource and risk data.
Compensation: Paid per validated report. Reports are archived only; retrieval of personnel is not guaranteed.

9.name: Surukan
9.description: A realm of serene silence. Every citizen focuses solely on themselves.
9.planetneed:
Position: Boundary Keeper
Duties: Protect personal boundaries
Requirements: Fair

10.name: Tarasus
10.description: An ocean of trees. Countless roots intertwine and fuse together, united as one.
10.planetneed: Roots entwined with roots — though they occasionally tangle and twist. The old village chief has stiff legs and speaks slowly. We're short a translator who can listen to both sides of the tree-root conversations.
